/insights/a-3-minute-implementation-guid.title: A 3 Minute Implementation Guide to Segmenting AI Traffic in GA4 - Found Search Marketing
/insights/a-3-minute-implementation-guid.description: Segmenting AI search traffic in GA4 allows you to see traffic trends from answer engines over time and analyze correlations between other traffic sources (for example, organic search). This change in Google Analytics is very straightforward and should not take more than 3 minutes to implement.

/insights/author/julie/.title: Julie Warnecke, Author at Found Search Marketing

@graph.name: Julie Warnecke, Author at Found Search Marketing
Julie Warnecke
@graph.description: Julie Warnecke is the Founder and CEO of Found Search Marketing, a Midwest based data engineering firm that activates paid media strategies. After getting her start at Google as one of the first employees on the AdWords team, Julie helped launch initiatives that shaped the future of paid media and search. This included AdWords Select, a groundbreaking CPC platform, as well as the industry-changing AOL syndication deal. She was also a key figure in helping open Google’s Paris office in 2002. In 2006, Julie returned to the Midwest with a mission: build a new kind of agency that serves as a seamless extension of existing client teams. Found was born from that idea, and it continues to fuel the company’s success today. Under Julie’s leadership, Found has grown to more than 50 employees, manages more than $200M in media spend annually, and maintains a 95% client retention rate.

@graph.name: TR Scrivner, Author at Found Search Marketing
TR Scrivner
@graph.description: As a long time member of the team, TR is focused on graphic design for a variety of clients and is continuously learning new techniques. He spends each day developing different types of creative, from paid media display graphics to motion graphics videos. T.R.’s favorite thing about FoundSM is the flexibility and ability to thrive as a self-starter. When he is not at work, he can be found drifting, playing video games, riding his bike, and spending time at the beach (when he is not in Indiana).

/insights/authors/adam/.description: Adam joined Found Search Marketing in 2016, leveraging his media strategy background to drive client results through research and testing. A Brown University graduate in Cognitive Neuroscience and Economics, he began his career with the Indianapolis Colts. Outside the office, Adam stays active through CrossFit, backpacking, and various team sports.

@graph.name: Julie Warnecke
@graph.description: Julie Warnecke is the Founder and CEO of Found Search Marketing, a Midwest based data engineering firm that activates paid media strategies. After getting her start at Google as one of the first employees on the AdWords team, Julie helped launch initiatives that shaped the future of paid media and search. This included AdWords Select, a groundbreaking CPC platform, as well as the industry-changing AOL syndication deal. She was also a key figure in helping open Google’s Paris office in 2002. In 2006, Julie returned to the Midwest with a mission: build a new kind of agency that serves as a seamless extension of existing client teams. Found was born from that idea, and it continues to fuel the company’s success today. Under Julie’s leadership, Found has grown to more than 50 employees, manages more than $200M in media spend annually, and maintains a 95% client retention rate.

@graph.name: TR Scrivner
@graph.description: As a long time member of the team, TR is focused on graphic design for a variety of clients and is continuously learning new techniques. He spends each day developing different types of creative, from paid media display graphics to motion graphics videos. T.R.’s favorite thing about FoundSM is the flexibility and ability to thrive as a self-starter. When he is not at work, he can be found drifting, playing video games, riding his bike, and spending time at the beach (when he is not in Indiana).

/whitepapers/first-party-data/.title: Why First-Party Data Is Your Most Valuable Profit Lever
/whitepapers/first-party-data/.description: In this comprehensive technical guide, you’ll learn how to architect a closed-loop data system that feeds real revenue intelligence (not noisy browser signals) back into your ad platforms. You’ll discover how to capture durable identifiers, store them in a resilient first-party framework, and push enriched, truth-based events directly into Google, Meta, LinkedIn, and beyond.
